## INTENDED COMPLETE PERSONA FEATURE SET

**YARDSTICK: Every bullet describes what "fully wired" means — i.e., the path from a real entrypoint (route/scheduler/channel/agent dispatch) reaches the user or the model.**

- **Persona record / body (PersonaStore)** — `personas.sqlite` per-row `system_prompt`; "fully wired" = CRUD reachable via `GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /admin/personas` (routes_admin_a/studio/personas.py:17-24) and body auto-seeded on first boot via `seed_builtin_personas` called in entrypoint.py:329. **STATUS: wired.** Grantley body seeded; CRUD exposed.

- **Persona↔channel binding + system-prompt injection** — `PUT /admin/channels/{channel}/humanlike` persists `(humanlike_enabled, persona_id)` to config; channel handlers call `inject_persona_if_enabled` (persona_inject.py:137) which prepends `system_prompt` as leading `role="system"` message; "fully wired" = changing the toggle takes effect on the very next inbound message without restart. **STATUS: wired** for QQ, Telegram, Discord, Slack, Feishu (service.py:1437-1440, 2596-2599, 3413-3416, 3712-3715, 4003).

The scheduler builtins (`qzone_daily.py`, `evolution_darwin_curate.py`) do not reference decay. Fatigue stays at 0.0, mood stays neutral forever on any fresh install (confirmed by VPS: all 14 rows `mood=neutral`, `state_json={}`).

findings.dimension: persona record CRUD + creation wizard end-to-end
findings.summary: PersonaStore CRUD is fully wired via admin_a routes (GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /admin/personas) and auto-seeded at boot via seed_builtin_personas in entrypoint.py:329. The configure-persona SKILL.md wizard's full tool chain — persona_create, persona_update, persona_life_set_seeds, persona_attach_asset_from_url, persona_list_assets — all exist as concrete dispatchers in agent_servicer.py with correct dispatch branches, and /persona is a registered command in COMMAND_REGISTRY with a wizard_prelude. Three high-severity gaps remain: (1) the grantley agent_persona_state row is never boot-seeded so all {{persona.*}} placeholders resolve to "" until the model explicitly calls persona_life_set_state; (2) mood/fatigue decay CLI exists but no server scheduler or lifespan task calls it so mood is permanently neutral; (3) EvolutionApplier.apply in both the gateway and auto-rollback packages only writes audit/history rows and never mutates any SKILL.md file or personas.sqlite body — the self-evolution apply chain is bookkeeping-only. Lycaon and vivian personas are pure VPS-side runtime data; neither is bundled in the repo — this is a content gap only, not a code gap. The grantley body in the repo (175 lines, 8494 chars) is ahead of the VPS prod row (3270 chars) by exactly the {{persona.*}} live-state block, which itself is inert until the state row is seeded.

gaps.title: seed_builtin_personas never overwrites — VPS prod row lacks {{persona.*}} live-state block
gaps.detail: seed_builtin_personas is idempotent: if the grantley row already exists it returns immediately without updating it (store.py:418-423). The repo body (default_grantley.md, 175 lines) adds a '## 此刻的我' section with 8 {{persona.*}} placeholders that the VPS prod row (3270 chars, no such section) lacks. Since the seeder never overwrites, deploying a new version does not propagate the updated body to prod. Operators must manually PATCH the row via /admin/personas/grantley or delete the row to let the seeder re-insert.

findings.dimension: life-state engine (persona_life_* tools + agent_persona_state store + life_seeds/grantley.yaml + decay + placeholder/diary surfacing)
findings.summary: The life-state engine is fully built and correctly wired for MODEL-INITIATED operation, but nothing DRIVES it autonomously and the persona-state row is never seeded at boot. All six persona_life_* tools (get/set_state/diary_add/event_seed/get_seeds/set_seeds) are implemented in life.py, registered in PERSONA_LIFE_TOOLS, advertised via persona_life_tool_schemas() (agent_servicer.py:558), and dispatched (agent_servicer.py:2854-2921) against a lazily-opened agent_state.sqlite store (agent_servicer.py:3802-3834). set_state correctly mirrors mood to the native column, pushes activity to recent_topics, and mirrors life_* flat keys into state_json for the {{persona.*}} resolver. State is keyed (tenant_id="default", agent_id=persona_id) and the keying is CONSISTENT end-to-end: the channel stamps request.persona_id (persona_inject.py:218) -> proto ChatStart.persona_id field 13 -> extra["persona_id"] (agent_servicer.py:4530) -> _context_metadata stamps md["agent_id"] from persona_id (agent_servicer.py:4321-4324) -> _CtxIdResolverAdapter reads ctx.metadata["agent_id"] (_engine_factory.py:60-63) -> PersonaResolver.resolve(key, agent_id=persona_id) reads store.get(persona_id) with the same default tenant. IMPORTANT CORRECTION TO THE PRIOR CAVEAT: _context_metadata DOES now stamp agent_id from persona_id (line 4322-4323) — the placeholder path is not dead in code. THE TWO REAL DEFECTS: (1) there is NO autonomous driver of any kind — exhaustive search finds zero call sites for apply_decay/decay-once/_run_decay anywhere in server/channels/agent; the only four scheduler builtins (qzone_daily, evolution_darwin_curate, system_update_check, registry) never reference decay or persona_life; there is no post-turn hook and no lifespan create_task loop. Life advances ONLY if the model happens to call the tools, and mood/fatigue/recent_topics never decay toward baseline. (2) No boot-time seeder creates the persona-state row — seed_from_card (corlinman-persona/seeder.py:116) is referenced only in a doc comment (subagent/runner.py:1038) and is never invoked from the server lifecycle, so on a fresh deploy no 'grantley' row exists until the model first calls set_state; until then every {{persona.*}} life placeholder AND the qzone daily life block resolve to "". Diary is surfaced ONLY by the opt-in qzone daily job (qzone_daily.py:670-685) and via an explicit persona_life_get tool call — it is NOT read back into normal channel conversation context and is not exposed by the resolver or any UI. Verdict: engine exists and is wired for model-driven use, but nothing drives it forward over time and the surfacing path is empty until the model bootstraps the row itself.

gaps.detail: There is no scheduled life-tick, no daily life advance, no on-message life update, and no decay sweep wired into any runtime entrypoint. apply_decay (decay.py) and the decay-once CLI (cli.py) exist and are correct but are never invoked by the server, scheduler, or any lifespan task. findings.summary: The {{persona.*}} resolution CHAIN is fully wired and correctly keyed in the current code — contradicting the audit's "KNOWN CAVEAT". _context_metadata (agent_servicer.py:4315-4326) DOES stamp md["agent_id"], falling back to extra["persona_id"]; that metadata flows ctx.metadata["agent_id"] → _CtxIdResolverAdapter (_engine_factory.py:62,214) → PersonaResolver.resolve(key, agent_id) → store.get(agent_id) against agent_state.sqlite. The persona namespace is registered (build_default_engine reads state.persona_resolver published by _wire_c2_handles at entrypoint.py:369, which runs BEFORE serve_placeholder_in_background at entrypoint.py:1132). So the keying is NOT the break. The REAL #1 break is at the terminal lookup: agent_state.sqlite has no row keyed (tenant_id="default", agent_id="grantley"). PersonaResolver.resolve returns "" when store.get returns None (placeholders.py:97-99). No boot seeder ever creates that row — seed_from_card exists (corlinman-persona/seeder.py:116) and is exported but is NEVER called by the server lifecycle; the only writers are the model-invoked persona_life_* tools. VPS confirms 14 rows keyed model::agent-type::idx, zero keyed grantley. DEFINITIVE VERDICT: {{persona.mood}}, {{persona.fatigue}}, {{persona.recent_topics}}, {{persona.life_*}} all SILENTLY RENDER EMPTY for grantley at runtime — not because of mis-keying, but because no grantley state row is ever seeded (and the model rarely/never calls the life tools first). A secondary precondition is that the channel must actually pass a non-empty persona_id (chat_service.py:430), which only happens when humanlike injection is enabled for that channel.

findings.summary: Mood/fatigue/recent_topics evolution is structurally designed but effectively dead code in production. The decay logic (apply_decay in decay.py) and the CLI sweep (_run_decay / decay-once in cli.py) are correct and unit-tested, but no server-side scheduler, lifespan task, or registered default job ever calls them. The persona_decay cron entry exists only in docs/config.example.toml as an opt-in subprocess job; a VPS with the default config never fires it. Mood is only updated when the model explicitly passes a 'mood' field to persona_life_set_state; fatigue is never incremented by any code path at all (update_fatigue exists in PersonaStore but has zero call sites); recent_topics only grows when the model calls set_state with a non-empty activity. All three fields are therefore permanently at their defaults (mood='neutral', fatigue=0.0, recent_topics=[]) on every production install — exactly what the VPS audit found across all 14 rows. The placeholder resolver (PersonaResolver / PlaceholderEngine) is correctly wired via c2_wiring.py and _engine_factory.py, but resolves to empty strings for all {{persona.*}} tokens because no boot-time seeder creates the grantley row in agent_persona_state (seed_from_card is never called from the server boot path, and the state row only appears on the first model tool call). The root causes are two independent gaps: (1) missing boot-time state row seeding for grantley, and (2) missing scheduled/background trigger for the decay sweep. Both must be fixed together before the evolution system produces any observable effect.

The QZone publish and comment tools are structurally complete and wired into BUILTIN_TOOLS (agent_servicer.py:332,341). The daily-publish scheduler path — daily_job.json template seeding (app_factory.py:580), admin enable route (scheduler.py:246), builtin registry registration (qzone_daily.py:896), cron tick loop dispatch (runner.py:469-546) — is fully reachable from a real entrypoint. However, three end-to-end gaps break the intended daily-post experience: (1) qzone_daily._build_internal_chat_request never sets persona_id on InternalChatRequest, so the agent turn carries no persona binding and image generation runs without 立绘 asset references; (2) life-state context injected by _resolve_life_block always returns None on any fresh deploy because no grantley row exists in agent_persona_state (same root cause as the broader persona-state gap — see dimension Persona System audit); (3) the CORLINMAN_NAPCAT_HTTP_URL env var must be set or NapCat's ws_url must be derivable, and there is no error surface when it is absent at server startup — the failure only appears at tool-call time.

findings.dimension: self-evolution loop (the "hermes self-evolution" port): darwin/nuwa/curator → signal → proposal → shadow/eval → apply → rollback, and whether it ever touches the PERSONA body
findings.summary: The self-evolution loop is a SKILL-quality loop, not a persona-evolution loop, and even the skill loop is only half-wired. The observe→propose half partially runs: the darwin curator scans SKILL.md quality on an auto-registered daily scheduler job (evolution.darwin_curate at 03:30 UTC, run_tool builtin) and writes skill.quality.issue signals to evolution.sqlite; admin can also fire it via the curator routes. But the propose half (EvolutionEngine.run_once that clusters signals into proposals) has NO server-side trigger — it only runs as a subprocess scheduler job that is a commented EXAMPLE in config.example.toml, never auto-registered, so on a default deploy no proposals are ever minted from darwin signals. The apply→shadow→rollback half is effectively dead: (1) the route's applier (corlinman_auto_rollback.EvolutionApplier) only flips proposal status rows + writes an audit/history row + intent-log; it explicitly does NOT mutate any SKILL.md, prompt, kb.sqlite, or personas.sqlite (its own docstring says "the kb/filesystem mutators stay a follow-up"); (2) the diff a darwin proposal carries is a __DARWIN_REPORT__ placeholder that parses as a unified diff but encodes no real change; (3) shadow gating cannot run — corlinman-shadow-tester has no console-script so the example cron would spawn_failed; (4) AutoRollbackMonitor has no runtime driver anywhere (the server's own `rollback run-once` CLI is a todo_stub; the only real entry is the corlinman-auto-rollback CLI which is again only an opt-in example cron, disabled by default); and (5) even if the monitor ran, watched_event_kinds() returns a non-empty whitelist ONLY for memory_op — darwin/skill_update/prompt_template/tool_policy/agent_card all get an empty tuple and are silently skipped, so no skill-quality proposal could ever auto-rollback. The ONE path that actually mutates files from a live channel is the user-correction → background_review LLM fork, which writes SKILL.md/MEMORY.md/USER.md (never persona body), and the curator /run route, which writes only lifecycle STATE (active/stale/archived) + pin flags into SKILL.md frontmatter (not content). Signal emitters: EvolutionObserver (hook-bus adapter) + darwin_curator + curator + user-correction listener. Proposal minter: EvolutionEngine (untriggered). Applier driver: only the manual admin /apply route. PERSONA-EVOLUTION VISION STATUS: entirely unmigrated/dead — nothing in darwin, the engine handlers, the appliers, or nuwa targets personas.sqlite system_prompt or life/seeds; nuwa exists only as a bundled SKILL.md with no in-process tool path. Note: the baseline's claim that the route applier uses metrics_baseline={} is STALE — that empty-baseline applier is gateway/evolution/applier.py, which the route does NOT use; the route uses corlinman_auto_rollback.EvolutionApplier which captures a real (but for non-memory_op kinds, empty-counts) baseline. The net effect (dead rollback chain) is the same.

gaps.title: EvolutionObserver is never instantiated or .start()-ed in the running gateway

It lists corlinman_server.gateway.evolution as a sibling 'evolution observer', but that package's __init__.py (gateway/evolution/__init__.py) defines NO bootstrap function — it only re-exports symbols. A grep for `def bootstrap` under gateway/evolution returns nothing, and `EvolutionObserver(` is never constructed anywhere outside tests. So no tool.call.failed/timeout/approval.rejected/subagent.* hook events are ever adapted into evolution_signals at runtime. The only signals that actually get written come from the darwin curator and the curator route (skill.quality.issue / curator EVENT_* rows) and the user-correction listener.
gaps.fix_sketch: Add `def bootstrap(state)` to gateway/evolution/__init__.py that constructs EvolutionObserver(bus=app.state.hook_bus, repo=SignalsRepo(evolution_store.conn)), calls .start(), and returns the background tasks; ensure a shared HookBus is published before the evolution sibling boots (today the bus is built later at entrypoint.py:1158-1166 only for the user-correction listener).

gaps.title: Apply is bookkeeping-only: NO skill/prompt/kb/persona file is ever mutated
gaps.detail: POST /admin/evolution/{id}/apply (evolution.py:502) constructs corlinman_auto_rollback.EvolutionApplier (applier.py:176) and calls .apply(). That path opens an intent_log row, captures a metrics baseline, writes an evolution_history audit row (before_sha/after_sha over the inverse_diff and the proposal.diff), and flips status to applied. gaps.title: Baseline doc claim is stale (metrics_baseline={} is the wrong applier)
gaps.detail: GAP_ANALYSIS_HERMES_OPENCLAW.md:86-88 and the audit baseline assert the route applier writes metrics_baseline={} (applier.py:279/340). That empty baseline belongs to gateway/evolution/applier.py:344, which the route does NOT use. The route's corlinman_auto_rollback.EvolutionApplier captures a real snapshot via _capture_baseline (applier.py:326-369). For non-memory_op kinds that snapshot has empty COUNTS but is still a valid monitor-decodable envelope (not bare {}). The downstream conclusion (rollback never fires for these kinds) is correct, but the stated cause is wrong.
gaps.fix_sketch: Update the docs to reflect the two distinct applier classes; the dead-rollback root cause is the empty watched_event_kinds whitelist + missing monitor driver, not metrics_baseline={}.

gaps.title: ShadowTester has no console-script and is never scheduled; no proposal ever reaches shadow_done
gaps.detail: corlinman-shadow-tester/pyproject.toml has NO [project.scripts] section (confirmed by grep), so the docs/config.example.toml:365-368 `command = corlinman-shadow-tester` cron would fail with spawn_failed even if an operator copied it in. docs/evolution-loop.md:83-94 carries a 'Reality check: Shadow gating is NOT wired' banner. The engine never imports/calls ShadowRunner; no proposal is ever flipped to shadow_running/shadow_done at runtime. [evolution.shadow].enabled defaults false.
gaps.fix_sketch: Add a [project.scripts] console entrypoint to corlinman-shadow-tester (e.g. corlinman-shadow-tester = corlinman_shadow_tester.cli:main) and an in-process or auto-registered scheduler job, then route risk>=medium proposals through it; today the design is documented but unimplemented at the wiring level.

gaps.title: No runtime driver: monitor never runs in the gateway
gaps.detail: AutoRollbackMonitor.run_once is only invoked by the corlinman-auto-rollback CLI (cli.py:287), which (a) hard-refuses unless [evolution.auto_rollback].enabled=true (cli.py:248) and (b) requires an --applier import-path (cli.py:256). That CLI is only an opt-in EXAMPLE cron (config.example.toml:376-379), not an auto-registered job. The server's own `corlinman rollback run-once` is a literal stub: cli/main.py:199 `todo_stub('rollback run-once')`. No lifespan task / scheduler builtin references the monitor. So the apply→monitor→rollback chain has no live trigger.
gaps.fix_sketch: Implement the `rollback run-once` server CLI (cli/main.py:197) or add an auto-registered in-process AutoRollbackMonitor tick (build monitor from app.state._evolution_store + the route's EvolutionApplier as the Applier) gated by [evolution.auto_rollback].enabled.

gaps.title: Even if driven, only memory_op can auto-rollback
gaps.detail: watched_event_kinds (metrics.py:146-157) returns a non-empty whitelist ONLY for EvolutionKind.MEMORY_OP ('tool.call.failed','search.recall.dropped'); every other kind (darwin, skill_update, prompt_template, tool_policy, agent_card) returns () and monitor.run_once skips it (monitor.py:144-151). So no SKILL-quality or prompt proposal could be metric-monitored or reverted regardless of driver.
gaps.fix_sketch: Extend watched_event_kinds with regression signal contracts for the other kinds (e.g. skill_update→skill.quality.issue trend) before relying on auto-rollback for anything beyond memory_op.

gaps.title: Curator mutates only SKILL.md lifecycle STATE + pin, never content or persona
gaps.detail: POST /admin/curator/{slug}/run (curator.py route:169) → _run_curator_now (dry_run=False) → maybe_run_curator → apply_lifecycle_transitions (_curator_logic.py:211) which sets skill.state and calls write_skill_md (_curator_logic.py:244-259). This is a real on-disk mutation but only flips active↔stale↔archived frontmatter (and the pin route flips pinned). It edits no skill body, no prompt, and nothing in personas.sqlite. Also gated to origin=='agent-created' skills (curator.py:238, _curator_logic.py:138), so bundled grantley-related skills are skipped. curator_state lives in evolution.sqlite via CuratorStateRepo (entrypoint.py:831).
gaps.fix_sketch: By design — note that this is lifecycle management, not the content-evolution the hermes vision implies; no fix needed unless content mutation is desired.

components.name: User-correction → background_review (the only live content-mutating path)

gaps.title: Writes SKILL.md / MEMORY.md / USER.md only — never the persona body; and provider resolver is stubbed
gaps.detail: register_user_correction_listener is wired in the lifespan (entrypoint.py:1219) and on a corrective chat phrase UserCorrectionApplier.apply (applier_user_correction.py:135) calls spawn_background_review (background_review.py:170), an LLM fork whose whitelisted tools write only MEMORY.md/USER.md (kind='memory') or create/patch SKILL.md (kind='skill') via corlinman_skills_registry safe-write (background_review.py:24-34). It cannot touch personas.sqlite system_prompt. Moreover the provider resolver is stubbed to (None, '') in the lifespan (_resolve_provider, entrypoint.py:1173-1182), so the applier short-circuits on resolver-failure — meaning even this path produces no LLM call / no write on a stock deploy until a real provider lookup is wired.
gaps.fix_sketch: Wire _resolve_provider (entrypoint.py:1173) to the real provider registry so background_review can actually fire; even then it targets skills/memory, not persona body, so it does not satisfy persona self-evolution.

components.name: nuwa-skill (autonomous skill creation) + persona-body evolution target

gaps.title: Persona body (personas.sqlite system_prompt / life / seeds) is NOT a target of any evolution path
gaps.detail: DEFAULT_HANDLERS (engine.py:80) covers memory_op, tag_rebalance, skill_update, darwin, prompt_template, tool_policy, agent_card — none target personas.sqlite. Darwin scans SKILL.md only (darwin.py header). prompt_template targets engine/config TOML prompts, not persona bodies. The auto-evolution design explicitly lists 'No personality/emotion-state mutation. That is a separate subsystem' as an anti-goal (docs/design/auto-evolution.md:358-359). So the 'self-evolving persona character' from the hermes vision is unmigrated/dead at every layer.
gaps.fix_sketch: Add a persona.quality_review signal kind + a curator/handler that scores persona body against a rubric and proposes PersonaStore.update() patches, plus an apply KindHandler that mutates personas.sqlite; treat as high-risk behind an explicit opt-in flag.

gaps.title: nuwa exists only as a bundled SKILL.md — no in-process tool path for autonomous skill/persona creation
gaps.detail: There is no nuwa handler in the evolution engine and no in-process tool that lets the agent autonomously create new skills or mutate the persona body; nuwa is just a SKILL.md prompt the model can follow with the ordinary skill-authoring tools. (Confirmed: no nuwa module under corlinman-evolution-engine; engine handler registry has no nuwa kind.) The closest live autonomous writer is background_review (skill/memory), reached only via user-correction, with a stubbed provider.
gaps.fix_sketch: If autonomous skill creation is desired, add a new_skill KindHandler (the design's risk='high' kind) with shadow+approval gating, or surface nuwa's create path as a gated agent tool; today it is documentation-only.

findings.summary: The persona->channel binding and system_prompt injection path is genuinely wired end-to-end for 5 of the project's channels (QQ/OneBot, Telegram, Discord, Slack, Feishu): an admin PUT to /admin/channels/{channel}/humanlike validates the persona exists, persists (humanlike_enabled, persona_id) into the channels config TOML, and mutates the same in-memory dict that each channel's live humanlike_resolver re-reads, so a toggle takes effect on the next inbound message without restart. On each inbound turn the channel handler calls inject_persona_if_enabled, which prepends the persona's system_prompt (plus an optional emoji block) as a leading role="system" message and stamps request.persona_id; that flows through ChatService -> _build_chat_start (chat_service.py:430) -> ChatStart.persona_id -> agent _to_agent_start extra["persona_id"] -> _context_metadata md["agent_id"]. Two material gaps: (1) the two "official" channels -- QQ Official and WeChat Official -- are deliberately excluded from persona binding entirely (no humanlike fields in their param classes, no persona_store passed at build time), so 2 of the 7 channels are NOT persona-aware; (2) the web /v1/chat/completions path performs NO persona injection at all -- _build_internal_request (chat.py:192-215) never sets persona_id and there is no humanlike layer, so 格兰 is never "alive" on web /chat. Notably, the baseline's "KNOWN CAVEAT" that _context_metadata does not stamp agent_id is OUTDATED: the current code (agent_servicer.py:4321-4323) explicitly falls back from extra["agent_id"] to extra["persona_id"], so the {{persona.*}} resolver IS keyed by persona_id for channel turns -- the real placeholder failure is the missing seeded agent_persona_state row (a separate dimension), not the metadata keying.

components.name: Admin humanlike binding route (PUT/GET /admin/channels/{channel}/humanlike) -- persist + validate

gaps.title: Baseline claim of 'no persona_id existence validation' is wrong -- validation IS present
gaps.detail: The baseline Component 2 gap asserts the PUT handler silently accepts non-existent persona_id (LOW). The current code at personas.py:405-414 looks up store.get(body.persona_id) and returns 404 persona_not_found if absent, AND at :396-403 enforces persona_id_required when enabled=true. So this gap is already closed in the code; the audited reality is stronger than the baseline states.
gaps.fix_sketch: No fix needed for validation. One residual: admin_a_state is constructed with persona_store=None at app_factory.py:614 and the store is wired later; if the PUT route's _persona_store(state) resolves None at request time, validation would degrade -- confirm the lifespan sets persona_store before serving traffic.

gaps.title: Injection no-ops silently when persona body is empty or store missing (graceful, not a defect)
gaps.detail: inject_persona_if_enabled returns without touching the request when enabled is false, persona_id is empty, persona_store is None, the persona row is missing, or system_prompt is blank (persona_inject.py:190-203). This is intentional graceful degradation, but it also means a stale/empty grantley body would silently disable in-character behavior with no operator signal.
gaps.fix_sketch: Add a one-time WARN log in inject_persona_if_enabled when humanlike is enabled with a persona_id but the resolved body is empty, so operators see why the bot is not in-character.

components.name: persona_id transport: channel request -> ChatService -> ChatStart -> agent extra -> placeholder agent_id

gaps.title: Baseline 'KNOWN CAVEAT' (that _context_metadata does NOT stamp agent_id) is OUTDATED
gaps.detail: The audit input states life/mood placeholders likely do not resolve because _context_metadata does not stamp agent_id. Current code disproves this: _context_metadata (agent_servicer.py:4321-4325) reads extra['agent_id'], and when absent FALLS BACK to extra['persona_id'] (set at 4530 from pb_start.persona_id) and stamps md['agent_id']. So for channel turns where injection set request.persona_id, the {{persona.*}} resolver IS keyed by the bound persona_id. The placeholders resolving to empty is therefore caused by the missing agent_persona_state row for that persona_id (a separate state-seeding dimension), NOT by metadata keying.
gaps.fix_sketch: No code change for keying. The downstream emptiness is fixed by seeding an agent_persona_state row keyed (tenant_id, agent_id=persona_id) at boot (separate component). For multi-persona deployments where agent_id != persona_id, consider preferring persona_id over agent_id when a humanlike-bound channel turn is detected.

components.name: QQ Official + WeChat Official channels -- persona binding NOT supported

gaps.title: 2 of 7 channels (qq_official, wechat_official) cannot bind a persona at all
gaps.detail: QqOfficialChannelParams (service.py:4114) and WeChatOfficialChannelParams (service.py:4559) carry only config/model/chat_service[/register_route] -- no humanlike_enabled/persona_id/persona_store/asset_store fields. Their handlers (handle_one_qq_official ~4382, handle_one_wechat_official ~4723) contain zero persona/inject references. Their param builders (_build_qq_official_params at channels_runtime:666, _build_wechat_official_params at :731) are called WITHOUT persona_store/asset_store. SUPPORTED_HUMANLIKE_CHANNELS = {qq, telegram, discord, slack, feishu} (_personas_lib.py:224) excludes them, and _validate_channel_name 404s any attempt to bind. The exclusion is documented as intentional in _personas_lib.py (WeChat is webhook-only; QQ Official does its own per-platform formatting). So 格兰 is silently NOT in-character on these two channels even though they relay to the same agent.
gaps.fix_sketch: Add humanlike_enabled/persona_id/persona_store/asset_store fields to QqOfficialChannelParams and WeChatOfficialChannelParams, call _inject_persona_if_enabled in both handlers before chat_service.run, pass persona_store/asset_store + _humanlike_resolver in _build_qq_official_params/_build_wechat_official_params, and add the two slugs to SUPPORTED_HUMANLIKE_CHANNELS.

gaps.title: Web /chat never selects or injects a persona; persona_id is always empty on the web path
gaps.detail: _build_internal_request (chat.py:192-215) constructs InternalChatRequest with model/messages/session_key/stream/max_tokens/temperature only -- it never sets persona_id and there is no humanlike toggle / persona_store lookup on the HTTP route. The only persona-adjacent preprocessing is rewrite_trailing_user_message (slash-command prelude swap), not system_prompt injection. Consequently _build_chat_start reads persona_id as '' (chat_service.py:430), no role=system persona message is prepended, and {{persona.*}} cannot resolve. There is no per-conversation persona binding configurable for web /chat. 格兰 is therefore NOT alive on the web chat surface unless the caller manually includes the persona body in messages.
gaps.fix_sketch: Introduce a web persona-binding source (e.g. a [web.humanlike] config block or a body/header persona_id) and, in _build_internal_request or handle_chat (chat.py:420), call inject_persona_if_enabled-equivalent against AppState.persona_store + asset_store to prepend the persona system message and set internal_req.persona_id before service.run. Pydantic InternalChatRequest already has a persona_id field, so the proto/agent path will carry it once populated.

findings.summary: The persona system has a real, functional web UI for the core persona CRUD and channel humanlike toggle at /admin/persona, plus a dedicated QZone daily job manager at /admin/scheduler/qzone. Both pages call real backend APIs. However, the persona life-state layer (mood, fatigue, diary, life seeds, recent topics) is entirely API-less and UI-less — there is no admin route, no React page, and no i18n key for viewing or editing the agent_persona_state rows. The /admin/scheduler/qzone page exists as a Next.js route under (admin)/scheduler/qzone/page.tsx but is NOT linked in the sidebar navigation (sidebar.tsx only lists /scheduler, not /scheduler/qzone), making it effectively unreachable to a non-developer unless they know the URL. The system is therefore partially operable by a non-developer: persona CRUD and channel binding are fully UI-accessible; life-state, mood/fatigue, diary, life seeds, and event-seed authoring are API-only or model-tool-only with no web surface whatsoever.

gaps.title: HIGH — No UI or API to view/edit persona life state (mood, fatigue, recent_topics, state_json)
gaps.detail: The agent_persona_state SQLite table (in agent_state.sqlite) holds the live mood, fatigue, recent_topics, and state_json for each persona. No admin route exposes this data. A non-developer (and even a developer) cannot inspect the current life state of grantley without shelling into the VPS and running sqlite3. This also means there is no way to manually seed the grantley state row from the UI (the row is never auto-created at boot; see Component 3 gap in the prior analysis).
gaps.fix_sketch: Add GET /admin/personas/{id}/life-state → read agent_persona_state row for agent_id=id; PATCH /admin/personas/{id}/life-state to manually seed or override; expose in PersonaEditorDialog as a collapsible 'Life state' section showing mood, fatigue, recent_topics JSON.

gaps.detail: Decay is only runnable via the CLI command. No admin route exposes it, and no scheduler job calls it. The UI has no surface for this. Combined with the missing state-row seed (Component 3 gap), mood and fatigue are perpetually neutral/0.0 on any fresh install.
